videos.title: Sick Infant Care
videos.description: How to care for your infant at home.

carePlan: WHO guidelines recommend the prescription of elemental iron and folic acid supplements for children with anemia.
WHO guidelines recommend the prescription of Post Discharge Malaria Chemoprevention for children with severe anaemia. (Hb <7 g/dL)
It is recommended to link the caregiver to nutritional programmes.
5000 IU vitamin A daily, either as an integral part of therapeutic foods or multi-micronutrient formulation.
It is recommended to link the caregiver to nutritional programmes.
5000 IU vitamin A daily, either as an integral part of therapeutic foods or multi-micronutrient formulation.
WHO guidelines recommend providing caregivers with 2-4 packets of Oral Rehydration Salts (ORS) for children with diarrhea to take home.

carePlan.text: Zinc: give 10 mg/day for 10-14 days for children 2 to 6 months of age.
Zinc: give 20 mg/day for 10-14 days for children over 6 months of age.

carePlan: WHO guidelines recommend providing caregivers with 2-4 packets of Oral Rehydration Salts (ORS) for children with diarrhea to take home.

carePlan.text: Zinc: give 10 mg/day for 10-14 days for children 2 to 6 months of age.
Zinc: give 20 mg/day for 10-14 days for children over 6 months of age.

carePlan: The WHO and national ministries of health recommend folic acid, penicillin/Erythromycin, Hydroxyurea, and sulfadoxine and pyrimethamine as essential medications for the standard components for the preventive care and management of sickle cell disease (SCD)
WHO guidelines recommend that children receive deworming every 6 months.

carePlan.text: It is recommended that children aged 1-2 years receive 200mg Albendazole or 500mg Mebendazole.
It is recommended that children over 2 years old receive 400mg Albendazole or 500mg Mebendazole.
